course.title: SQL Database Fundamentals
course.description: Learn SQL to query, manage, and analyze data in relational databases.

chapter.title: Relational Data and Joins
chapter.description: Learn how to combine data from multiple tables using JOIN operations.

lessons.title: Introduction to JOINs
objectives: Understand the concept of relational databases
Use INNER JOIN to combine rows from two tables
lessons.explanation: An `INNER JOIN` keyword selects records that have matching values in both tables. It is used to combine columns from one or more tables based on the values of the common columns between related tables.
lessons.syntax:
SELECT column_name(s)
FROM table1
INNER JOIN table2
ON table1.column_name = table2.column_name;

exercises.statement: Select the `name` from the `students` table and the `course_name` from the `enrollments` table using an INNER JOIN. The common column is `student_id`.
exercises.hint: Use SELECT students.name, enrollments.course_name FROM students INNER JOIN enrollments ON students.student_id = enrollments.student_id;
exercises.solution: SELECT students.name, enrollments.course_name FROM students INNER JOIN enrollments ON students.student_id = enrollments.student_id;

lessons.title: LEFT JOIN
objectives: Use LEFT JOIN to retrieve all records from the left table and matched records from the right
lessons.explanation: The `LEFT JOIN` keyword returns all records from the left table (table1), and the matching records from the right table (table2). The result is 0 records from the right side, if there is no match.
lessons.syntax:
SELECT column_name(s)
FROM table1
LEFT JOIN table2
ON table1.column_name = table2.column_name;

exercises.statement: Select the `name` from `customers` and `order_date` from `orders` using a LEFT JOIN. The common column is `customer_id`. (Alice has an order, Bob does not, but both should appear in the result!).
exercises.hint: Use LEFT JOIN instead of INNER JOIN.
exercises.solution: SELECT customers.name, orders.order_date FROM customers LEFT JOIN orders ON customers.customer_id = orders.customer_id;
